name: Macadamia Fudge Torte
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup low-fat sweetened condensed milk, not evaporated
1 pkg. Pillsbury Moist Supreme Devil's Food Cake Mix
16 ounces Sliced Pears in Light Syrup, drained
1/3 cup Chopped Macadamia Nuts or Pecans
17 ounce Jar Butterscotch Caramel Fudge Ice Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Spray a 9 or 10-inch springform pan with nonstick cooking spray.
3. In a small saucepan, combine filling ingredients. Cook over Medium-Low heat until chocolate is melted, stirring occasionally.
4. In a large bowl, combine cake mix, cinnamon and oil. Mix at Low speed for 20-30 seconds, or until crumbly. The mixture will be dry.
5. Place pears in blender container or food processor bowl with metal blade, cover and blend until smooth.
6. In a large bowl, combine 2 1/2 cups of the cake mix mixture, pureed pears and eggs. Mix at Low speed until moistened. Then beat for 2 minutes at Medium speed.
7. Spread batter evenly in spray-coated pan. Drop filling by spoonfuls over batter.
8. Stir nuts and water into remaining cake mix mixture. Sprinkle over filling.
9. Bake at 350°F for 45-50 minutes, or until the top springs back when touched lightly in the center. Cool for 10 minutes. Remove sides of pan. Cool completely for 1 1/2 hours, or until cooled.
10. In a small saucepan, combine sauce ingredients. Cook over Medium-Low heat for 3-4 minutes, or until well blended, stirring occasionally.
11. To serve, spoon 2 Tablespoons warm sauce onto each serving plate. Top with wedge of torte. If desired, serve with vanilla ice cream or frozen yogurt garnished with chocolate curls.
